meaning: n.[动]食火鸡(Casuarius,产于澳大利亚,新几内亚)
examples: Cassowary put his domed head close to Bower-bird's beak.
A cassowary looks like a flamboyant ostrich and can be found wandering the rain forests of Australia and New Guinea.
Unlike the ostrich and emu, to which it is related, the Cassowary is most at home in the rainforest where the vegetation is dense and it can disappear from view quickly.
The plantation's menagerie also includes rhinoceroses, the ostrichlike cassowary, cheetahs and giant eland, a type of large African antelope.
